meta.title: Global Bridge Consultancy — Evidence Structuring
meta.description: We design systems that transform fragmented information into organized, comparable, and traceable knowledge for responsible decision-making.

hero.tagline: We bridge the gap between data and informed decision-making.

philosophy.text: In a world where information is abundant but understanding is scarce, Global Bridge structures and transforms the scattered into clarity.

whatWeDo.title: What we do
whatWeDo.subtitle: Tailored solutions to structure evidence in complex environments.

capabilities.title: We collect information
capabilities.description: We identify, gather, and process data from multiple sources. Structured or not, we can process all types of information so it can be used coherently and in an orderly manner.

capabilities.title: We develop analysis methods
capabilities.description: To understand information systematically and reach evidence-based conclusions. Available methods include:
items: Comparative analysis between actors or positions
Detailed description of problems or findings

capabilities.title: We design visualization and presentation tools
capabilities.description: To understand, navigate, and explore information more clearly and communicate it effectively.

capabilities.title: We handle data appropriately
capabilities.description: Our goal is to build systems that are reliable, transparent, and objective.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Examples of systems designed to structure evidence and support decision-making.

items.title: Youth Electoral Monitor (Colombia 2026)
items.description: Interactive platform presenting findings from a national survey conducted by Cifras & Conceptos of 3,221 young people on political participation, electoral decisions, and democratic attitudes.
features: Visualizes electoral participation and preference patterns
Shows ideological distribution and government evaluations
Enables exploration of key paradoxes and trends
items.outcome: Structured access to Cifras & Conceptos survey data.

items.title: Presidential Evidence Assistant (Colombia 2026)
items.description: A question-and-comparison system over proposals and interviews, with traceable links to original sources.
features: Structures video interviews with timestamp references
items.outcome: Structured and verifiable access to public statements.

items.title: Electoral Results (Colombia 2026)
items.description: Interactive dashboard to explore presidential election results at national, departmental, and municipal levels, with analysis of strongholds and competitive territories.
features: Visualizes results by candidate at national and territorial levels
Shows most competitive departments and margin differences
items.outcome: Transparent access to official electoral results.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: If you work with complex information and need to structure it for analysis or decision-making, we can help.
contact.credentials: Founded by experts in data systems, AI, machine learning, governance, and institutional analysis.

footer.copyright: Global Bridge Consultancy. All rights reserved.

meta.title: Presidential Evidence Assistant — Global Bridge Consultancy
meta.description: A question-and-comparison system over proposals and interviews from Colombia's 2026 presidential candidates, with traceable links to original sources.

header.title: Presidential Evidence Assistant
header.subtitle: An intelligent system that lets users ask questions and compare proposals from more than 24 presidential candidates. It provides answers based on each candidate's government proposals and interviews, linking to the original source of each statement.

screenshotCaptions: Landing page — example queries and system boundaries clearly displayed
Multi-turn conversation with numbered citations and traceable source links

items.title: Traceable citations
items.body: Every claim carries a [N] reference linking to a YouTube video at the exact second, a PDF at the page number, or the original web page.

items.title: No opinions or rankings
items.body: The system never ranks candidates or says one proposal is better. It presents what each candidate said and lets the user decide.

items.title: Balanced retrieval
items.body: Comparisons guarantee fair representation — candidates with more published material don't dominate the results.

items.title: Transparent gaps
items.body: When information is missing, the system says so explicitly rather than fabricating or filling in gaps.

howItWorks.title: 1. Collect & structure
howItWorks.body: Campaign proposals, video transcripts (with timestamps), and media articles are parsed into ~800-character fragments, each tagged with candidate, topic, source URL, and precise location.

howItWorks.title: 2. Classify & search
howItWorks.body: Each query is classified into one of five modes (see below), then routed through metadata filtering, keyword matching with synonym expansion, and semantic search.

howItWorks.title: 3. Generate with citations
howItWorks.body: A language model generates a response where every claim must carry a numbered citation. No opinions, no rankings, and explicit disclosure when information is missing.

presidential-assistant.howItWorksSummary: We collect proposals, interviews, and media articles related to each candidate. The tool classifies the query, searches across sources, and generates a response with verifiable citations.
limitations: Uneven coverage: only 2 of 24 candidates have structured published proposals.
Transcription quality: automatic video transcriptions may contain errors.
Information changes over time: the system reflects what was published at the last data update.
Variable media coverage: some candidates have 3–4 outlets, others only one.

meta.title: ROLI Dashboard — Global Bridge Consultancy
meta.description: An interactive analytical tool for exploring Rule of Law Index indicators, comparing countries, and analyzing trends over time.

header.title: ROLI Dashboard
header.subtitle: Rule of Law Index — An interactive analytical tool for exploring indicators, comparing countries, and analyzing trends.

overview: Interactive analytical tool for exploring indicators, identifying trends, and comparing data spanning 10+ years across 140 countries. Developed for the World Justice Project.
Enables comparative analysis and effective communication for researchers, analysts, and policymakers examining governance indicators with evidence. Export findings for additional reports.

howItWorks.title: 1. Ingestion & normalization
howItWorks.body: We take the databases and normalize them (years and editions) for efficient querying. Each data point is linked to: country, region, factor, and time period.

howItWorks.title: 2. Interactive visualization
howItWorks.body: We developed the dashboard where users can filter and analyze factors by time, region, and country in panels.

howItWorks.title: 3. Export
howItWorks.body: Visualizations can be exported for later use. Designed for both quick queries and comprehensive comparative analysis.

sources.description: Data sourced from the World Justice Project's published Rule of Law Index datasets:

items.detail: Annual scores across 8 factors and 44 sub-factors
Groupings by region, income level, and governance type

limitations: Dependent on WJP publication schedule: data reflects the latest published edition of the index.
Index methodology changes between editions may affect comparability of some sub-factors over time.
Country coverage varies by year as new countries are added to the index.

meta.title: Statement Consistency Engine — Global Bridge Consultancy
meta.description: A system designed to analyze the temporal consistency of public statements, grouping them by theme and highlighting potential divergences.

header.title: Statement Consistency Engine
header.subtitle: A system designed to analyze the temporal consistency of public statements, grouping them by theme and highlighting potential divergences.

overview: The Statement Consistency Engine is designed to track and analyze public statements over time. It groups statements by theme, enables temporal comparison, and links quotes to their original context — allowing analysts to identify potential shifts or contradictions in an actor's public positions.
The system is designed to highlight divergences without editorial interpretation, preserving the original context and letting users draw their own conclusions.

howItWorks.title: Statement collection and classification
howItWorks.body: Public statements from multiple sources are ingested, cleaned, and automatically classified into thematic categories. Each statement is linked to its author, date, source, and original context.

howItWorks.title: Temporal comparison
howItWorks.body: The system enables side-by-side comparison of statements from the same actor on the same topic across different time periods. This allows analysts to track how positions evolve and identify potential inconsistencies.

howItWorks.title: Divergence signaling
howItWorks.body: When potential divergences are detected between statements on the same topic, the system highlights them for review — without assigning interpretation. Users can examine the original context of each statement to evaluate the significance of any changes.

sources.description: The system is designed to ingest statements from multiple public source types:

items.detail: Policy papers, official statements, and reports

limitations: Prototype stage: the system architecture is defined but has not yet been deployed with a full dataset.
Statement detection relies on accurate source attribution and dating, which may not always be available.
Divergence detection is pattern-based and does not assess intent or context beyond what is explicitly stated.
Coverage depends on the availability and accessibility of public records for the actors being tracked.

meta.title: Youth Electoral Monitor — Global Bridge Consultancy
meta.description: Visualization of Cifras & Conceptos survey data from 3,221 young Colombians on political participation, electoral decisions, and democratic attitudes.

header.title: Youth Electoral Monitor
header.subtitle: Visualization of national survey data collected by Cifras & Conceptos from 3,221 young Colombians aged 18-32 on political participation, electoral decisions, and democratic culture.

overview: The Youth Electoral Monitor presents findings from a national survey conducted by Cifras & Conceptos, examining political participation, electoral decisions, and democratic culture among 3,221 young Colombians aged 18-32. Cifras & Conceptos is a leading public opinion research and political analysis firm in Colombia.

The platform transforms data collected by Cifras & Conceptos into interactive visualizations, enabling exploration of electoral participation patterns, ideological distribution, democratic satisfaction, and government evaluation. Designed for researchers, journalists, and civic organizations.
screenshotCaptions: Landing page with highlighted statistic showing 81% turnout intention for 2026 presidential elections
Interactive visualization of key findings on youth democratic attitudes

howItWorks.title: 1. Survey collection & validation
howItWorks.body: Cifras & Conceptos administered 3,847 surveys between November-December 2025, yielding 3,221 valid responses with ±3% margin of error at 95% confidence. The sample covers 46 municipalities nationally.

howItWorks.title: 2. Data structuring & analysis
howItWorks.body: Survey responses on electoral participation, ideology, democratic satisfaction, and government evaluation are organized into thematic categories. Statistical distributions are calculated to identify patterns and paradoxes.

howItWorks.title: 3. Interactive visualization
howItWorks.body: The platform presents findings through interactive charts and highlighted statistics. Users explore key paradoxes: high electoral mobilization alongside democratic disenchantment, or presidential popularity without policy approval.

sources.description: All data presented on this platform was collected by Cifras & Conceptos through a national survey:

items.detail: Data collected by Cifras & Conceptos: voting intention, electoral decision clarity, and political preferences
Data collected by Cifras & Conceptos: democratic satisfaction and openness to non-democratic governance
Data collected by Cifras & Conceptos: presidential image, policy approval, and ideological self-identification

items.title: High turnout intent, low decision clarity
items.body: 81% plan to vote in the 2026 presidential elections, yet only 25% have decided their vote. High mobilization coexists with electoral uncertainty.

items.title: Centrist ideological lean
items.body: 58% identify as centrist, while left and right each represent 21%, revealing an ideologically moderate youth cohort.

items.title: Democratic disenchantment
items.body: Only 19% express satisfaction with democracy, 30% are open to non-democratic governments, and 28% believe democracy primarily benefits politicians, signaling a legitimacy crisis.

items.title: Image-approval gap
items.body: President Petro has a 53% favorable image but only 29% approve his performance. The 24-point gap indicates personal popularity without substantive policy support.

limitations: Point-in-time snapshot: data reflects attitudes between November-December 2025. Opinions may shift before the 2026 elections.
Geographic coverage: the survey covers 46 municipalities, not the entire national territory.
Stated vs. actual behavior: declared voting intention may differ from actual electoral behavior.
Self-reported ideology: left-center-right identification relies on respondents' self-perception, not objective criteria.

meta.title: Electoral Results Colombia 2026 — Global Bridge Consultancy
meta.description: Interactive dashboard to explore Colombia's 2026 presidential election results at national, departmental, and municipal levels.

header.title: Electoral Results Colombia 2026
header.subtitle: Interactive dashboard to explore presidential election results at national, departmental, and municipal levels. Visualize electoral strongholds, competitive territories, and vote distribution by candidate.

overview: The Electoral Results Dashboard presents a comprehensive interactive visualization of Colombia's 2026 presidential election results. It enables analysis of vote distribution at national, departmental, and municipal levels, identifying geographic patterns and key territories.

The platform facilitates comparative analysis between candidates, showing electoral strongholds (areas of strength), most competitive departments (closest margins), and geographic concentration of electoral advantages. Designed for researchers, journalists, and citizens interested in understanding electoral outcomes.

howItWorks.title: 1. Data ingestion & cleaning
howItWorks.body: Official data from the National Civil Registry is processed through a Medallion architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold), cleaning and normalizing records from each polling station nationwide.

howItWorks.title: 2. Territorial aggregation
howItWorks.body: Results are aggregated at national, departmental, and municipal levels, calculating vote totals, percentages, and winners by territory. Strongholds and competitive zones are identified.

howItWorks.title: 3. Interactive visualization
howItWorks.body: The dashboard presents data through interactive charts and maps, enabling exploration of results by candidate, territory comparison, and analysis of geographic voting patterns.

sources.description: Official electoral results data processed from public sources:

items.detail: Data from Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional)

limitations: First round data: the dashboard reflects first-round electoral results.
Manual updates: data is updated manually when the Civil Registry publishes new official results.
Source accuracy: precision depends on data published by official sources.
